'''Tyson Smoothy''' (born 7 July 1999) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|hk}} for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]]. 

He previously played in the [[NRL]] for the [[Melbourne Storm]] and the [[Brisbane Broncos]], where he won the [[2025 NRL grand final]].

==Background==
Smoothy was born in [[Toowoomba]], [[Queensland]] and raised on the [[Sunshine Coast, Queensland|Sunshine Coast]]. He was educated at [[Mountain Creek State High School]] and briefly Kawana Waters State College and played his junior rugby league for the [[Kawana Dolphins]] before being signed by the [[Brisbane Broncos]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.melbournestorm.com.au/news/2021/02/09/four-falcons-vying-for-storm-contracts/|title=Four falcons vying for Storm contracts|date=9 February 2021|website=Melbourne Storm}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.qt.com.au/news/broncos-bound-teenager-set-for-grand-final/3085379/|title=Broncos-bound teenager set for Grand Final|date=3 September 2016|newspaper=The Courier Mail|last1=Nolan|first1=Alex}}</ref>

==Playing career==
In 2015, Smoothy represented [[Queensland under-16 rugby league team|Queensland under-16]] in their win over New South Wales.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/2015/05/05/queensland-u16-team/|title=Queensland under 16 team|date=5 May 2015|website=QRL}}</ref> In 2017, he played for the [[Brisbane Broncos]] [[NRL Under-20s|under-20s]] side and captained [[Queensland under-18 rugby league team|Queensland under-18]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/2017/06/12/queensland-under-18-team/|title=Queensland Under 18 team|date=12 June 2017|website=QRL}}</ref>

In 2018, Smoothy joined the [[Sunshine Coast Falcons]] in the [[Queensland Cup]], playing for their under-20 side. In 2019, he was named Man of the Match in their under-20 Grand Final win over the [[Wynnum Manly Seagulls]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.sunshinecoastnews.com.au/2021/03/09/sudden-rise-to-nrl-for-tyson-smoothy/|title=Smoothy and Loiero named on extended bench|date=9 March 2021|website=Sunshine Coast News}}</ref><ref name="JaiSmoothy">{{cite web |last1=Edwards |first1=Colleen |title=Things are going smoothly for Sunshine Coast's Smoothy |url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/2022/05/11/things-are-going-smoothly-for-sunshine-coasts-smoothy/ |website=qrl.com.au |publisher=[[Queensland Rugby League]] |access-date=27 June 2022 |date=11 May 2022 |quote=Tyson Smoothy accepts player of the match honours in the 2019 Hastings Deering Colts grand final}}</ref> In 2020, Smoothy joined the [[Penrith Panthers]], playing for them in the [[NRL Nines]] and the [[New South Wales Cup]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.penrithpanthers.com.au/news/2020/03/01/panthers-chased-down-by-western-suburbs/|title=Panthers chased down by Western Suburbs|date=1 March 2020|website=Penrith Panthers}}</ref>

===2021===
In 2021, Smoothy joined the [[Melbourne Storm]] on a train-and-trial deal.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.couriermail.com.au/news/queensland/sunshine-coast/door-left-open-for-tyson-smoothy-to-make-nrl-debut/news-story/13271748d72d37e6d1c0aab1244198d3|title=Door left open for Tyson Smoothy to make NRL debut|date=3 March 2021|website=The Courier Mail}}</ref>

In Round 1 of the [[2021 NRL season]], he made his NRL debut for the Melbourne club in their 26–18 win against [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|South Sydney]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.melbournestorm.com.au/news/2021/03/11/storm-v-rabbitohs-grant-finucane-out-bunnies-unveil-new-recruits/|title=Storm v Rabbitohs: Grant, Finucane out; Bunnies unveil new recruits|date=11 March 2021|website=Melbourne Storm}}</ref> On 6 October, he was released by the Melbourne club, having played only four games in the [[2021 NRL season]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2021-moses-suli-to-the-dragons-release-contract-manly-sea-eagles-departing-players-curtis-sironen-tevita-funa-zac-saddler-jack-gosiewski-signings-tracker/news-story/c2a4522f0589bb56031ee7d8e44680cb/|title=Dragons confirm Suli deal as Manly land Bronco and re-sign pair: NRL Transfer Centre|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=6 October 2021}}</ref>

===2022===
Smoothy rejoined [[Sunshine Coast Falcons]] on full-time basis before being temporarily recalled by [[Melbourne Storm]] in Round 3 of the [[2022 NRL season]] when their four first choice hookers were unavailable.<ref>{{cite web |last1=O'Loughlin |first1=Liam |title=Craig Bellamy reveals the full extent of Melbourne Storm's hooker crisis |url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/craig-bellamy-reveals-full-extent-melbourne-storms-hooker-crisis/grplcdriit7szqudq3rwp2cl |website=Sporting News |access-date=14 April 2022 |date=25 March 2022}}</ref> He was named as a reserve, but was not required.<ref>{{cite web |title=Storm v Eels - Round 3, 2022 |url=https://www.nrl.com/draw/nrl-premiership/2022/round-3/storm-v-eels/ |website=NRL.com |publisher=[[National Rugby League]] |access-date=14 April 2022}}</ref>

At the end of the [[2022 Queensland Cup]] season, Smoothy was named as hooker in the competition's team of the season.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Hillier |first1=Michael |title=Tuaupiki and Storch take top honours at QRL Awards |url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/2022/09/23/tuaupiki-and-storch-take-top-honours-at-qrl-awards/ |website=qrl.com.au |access-date=25 September 2022 |date=23 September 2022}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |last1=Piepers |first1=David |title=Red-hot Dolphins recruit continues rapid rise, Carrigan, Storch honoured at QRL awards |url=https://www.zerotackle.com/red-hot-dolphins-recruit-continues-rapid-rise-carrigan-storch-honoured-at-qrl-awards-123375/ |website=Zero Tackle |access-date=26 September 2022 |date=24 September 2022}}</ref>

===2023===
Smoothy made his club debut for the [[Brisbane Broncos]] in his side's 24−20 victory over the [[Newcastle Knights]] in round 15 of the [[2023 NRL season]]. He played a total of 13 games for Brisbane in the [[2023 NRL season]]. He played in Brisbane's 26-24 loss against Penrith in the [[2023 NRL Grand Final]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2023/oct/01/nrl-grand-final-2023-penrith-panthers-vs-brisbane-broncos-live-updates-scores-results-teams-squads-kick-off-time-accor-stadium-sydney/|title=NRL grand final 2023: Penrith Panthers defeat Brisbane Broncos – as it happened|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

===2024===
Smoothy scored his first NRL try in the Brisbane Broncos in his side's 38-12 victory over the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] in round 4 of the [[2024 NRL season]]. He played 18 games for Brisbane in the 2024 NRL season which saw the club miss the finals, finishing 12th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2024-daly-cherryevans-and-cameron-munster-support-kevin-walters-facing-the-axe-brisbane-broncos-club-review-news-videos-highlights/news-story/a1e007cfb303202b87ff3a8eafde4060/|title=‘Really can’t hide’: Queensland superstars back under-siege Walters amid Broncos review|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref>

=== 2025 ===
On 13 February, it was announced that Smoothy would depart the Brisbane club at the end of the season to take up a three-year deal in the [[Super League]] with [[Wakefield Trinity]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Chalk |first=Ethan Lee |date=2025-02-12 |title=Broncos hooker set to depart at season's end |url=https://www.zerotackle.com/broncos-hooker-set-to-depart-at-seasons-end-218940/ |access-date=2025-02-14 |website=Zero Tackle |language=en-US}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |title=Brisbane Broncos hooker Tyson Smoothy completes Super League move for 2026 – Total Rugby League |url=https://www.totalrl.com/brisbane-broncos-hooker-tyson-smoothy-completes-super-league-move-for-2026/ |access-date=2025-02-14 |language=en-US}}</ref> Smoothy started week three of the [[2025 NRL finals series]] at lock filling in for the suspended [[Patrick Carrigan]] in a 16-14 win helping the Brisbane side make the [[2025 NRL grand final]]. On 5 October, Smoothy was part of the 17 man squad that won the [[2025 NRL grand final]], ending a 19 year premiership drought.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2025/oct/05/nrl-2025-grand-final-melbourne-storm-v-brisbane-broncos-rugby-league-live/|title=Brisbane Broncos beat Melbourne Storm – as it happened|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

===Honours===
'''Individual'''
* '''[[List of Queensland Cup honours#Team of the Year|Queensland Cup Team Of The Year Hooker]]''': 2022
'''Team'''
* '''[[Sunshine Coast Falcons|Queensland Cup Minor-Premiership]]''': 2019
* '''[[NRL Grand Final|NRL Grand Finalist]]''': 2023, 2025
* '''[[Brisbane Broncos#Honours|NRL Pre-Season Challenge Winner]]''': 2024, 2025
* '''[[2025 NRL grand final|NRL Premiership]]''': 2025
